objective: Make Vision issue reconciliation deterministic so batch merges do not create false-red workflows, stale lifecycle state, or search-index-dependent parity failures.
claim_ceiling: Prepared E0 only: the exact base, reviewer, event corpus, writable paths, negative controls, and integration-lease requirement are frozen. No workflow, issue, label, body, lifecycle, validator, or retry behavior changes yet. A future accepted D12 E0 result may repair only GitHub issue reconciliation and standalone merge-hook routing; it cannot weaken Vision parity or authorize application, data, factor, backtest, release, or Production work.

requirements: the queued registration is merged on main before preparation pins the activation base, independent reviewer, frozen event corpus, and integration lease
batch-managed PRs exit the standalone close hook before standalone Work-Key validation while complete-on-merge standalone PRs remain fail-closed
post-write parity refetches the unfiltered authoritative issue collection and filters scope:vision locally without label-search indexing
one repository concurrency group serializes reconciliation and terminal batch lifecycle is projected deterministically
concurrent human edits, pagination, retries, rate limits, partial failures, and reruns are either proven safe by executable tests or remain explicitly outside the accepted claim

later_claim_dependencies: Historical failed workflow attempts remain immutable; D12 prevents recurrence and reruns only attempts whose original event and workflow revision can still produce valid evidence.
Any broader GitHub governance redesign, application behavior, or delivery-policy relaxation requires separate authorization.

corpus.environment: Local/CI deterministic GitHub event and lifecycle fixtures only; no live issue mutation is authorized during preparation.
required_strata: merged managed-by-batch PR closure skips standalone issue completion without error
merged complete-on-merge standalone PR closes exactly its declared issue and malformed metadata fails closed
newly scoped issues are visible to post-write parity without label-search indexing
done and cancelled batches close with deterministic reasons while queued, prepared, and active batches remain open
concurrent edits, pagination, retries, rate limits, partial failures, and idempotent reruns

source_ceiling: GitHub issue and pull-request metadata only; no credential beyond the workflow token and no application, database, object-store, source, scheduler, release, consumer, or Production data operation is authorized.

release_activation.reason: D12 changes delivery metadata reconciliation only and cannot activate an application, source, runtime, schedule, consumer, release, or data flow.

negative_controls: closing a batch Work-Issue through the standalone hook or accepting a malformed standalone Work-Key
using a label-filtered GitHub search immediately after reconciliation writes
overlapping reconciliation writers or nondeterministic terminal issue state
overwriting a concurrent human edit, silently dropping a partial failure, or claiming untested retry and rate-limit safety
weakening scope:vision parity or changing application, data, factor, backtest, release, or Production behavior

allowed_evidence_readers: repository CI and governance operations planning only until a later accepted E0 result

invalidation: Changing event routing, lifecycle policy, managed labels, body ownership, concurrency, API consistency, retry policy, or corpus requires a new prepared revision before implementation.
Any attempt to weaken live parity or modify application, data, factor, backtest, release, or Production surfaces requires separate authorization.
rollback: Revert only the future D12 reconciliation workflow, tests, evidence, and lease; do not rewrite historical Actions runs or mutate application, data, factor, backtest, release, or Production state.
